name: Precision Product
description: A product-grade profile for developer tools, AI systems, dashboards, and open-source launch websites that need rigor, proof, and restrained visual confidence.
licenseNotes: Source-backed principles only. Do not clone proprietary product surfaces, brand assets, copy, or screenshots.
principles: Lead with what exists: commands, generated files, screenshots, audits, docs, and source paths.
Use restraint as a quality signal; decoration must clarify hierarchy, state, proof, or brand posture.
Vary section rhythm with architecture diagrams, proof surfaces, docs, specimens, and critique comparisons.
Documentation is part of the product surface, not an afterthought.
Every external source must become a principle or contract, not a copied visual.
positiveExamples: A hero that stages a real product surface and links to docs.
A docs page with quickstart, file anatomy, CLI commands, agent workflow, quality gates, and publishing guidance.
A proof section that references generated reports instead of fake traction metrics.
antiPatterns: Centered AI hero with abstract gradient and no product artifact.
Three identical feature cards that could describe any SaaS product.
Generic phrases like Elevate, Unleash, Seamless, Next-gen, and Transform your workflow.
Fake metrics or unverifiable reliability claims.
Open-source launch page without install commands, docs, or repository proof.
typographyRules: Use assertive editorial-scale headings only for true page or section leads.
Use compact, readable body copy with clear line-length limits.
Use monospaced text for commands, source paths, and proof artifacts.
Never use tiny labels as a substitute for hierarchy.
layoutRules: Hero must show real product proof or generated artifact evidence.
Alternate narrative, proof, architecture, docs, specimen, and critique sections.
Avoid card grids unless each card has a distinct role.
Mobile must preserve hierarchy and proof without horizontal overflow.
componentRules: Primary actions must be semantic links or buttons with visible focus and at least 44px targets.
Proof cards must include concrete nouns: command, report, screenshot, route, token, source.
External widgets, including GitHub stars, need readable fallback text.
copyRules: Prefer specific implementation nouns over hype verbs.
State what the system does, what file proves it, and how to run it.
Do not claim world-class taste without showing source-backed contracts or evidence.
screenshotNotes: Screenshots should show real generated output, not placeholder dashboards.
Use responsive screenshots to validate hierarchy and rhythm at mobile, tablet, and desktop sizes.
